data.title: BlackRock Acquires $900 Million in Bitcoin in One Week
data.description: BlackRock has reportedly purchased approximately $900 million worth of Bitcoin within a single week. According to ChainCatcher, blockchain analytics platform Arkham monitored the transactions, revealing that BlackRock's ETF clients currently have an estimated average holding cost of $87,000 per Bitcoin.

data.title: Hyperliquid Platform Whale Positions Reach $3.575 Billion
data.description:
Hyperliquid platform whales currently hold positions totaling $3.575 billion, according to ChainCatcher. Data from Coinglass reveals that long positions account for $1.849 billion, representing 51.72% of the total, while short positions amount to $1.726 billion, making up 48.28%. Long positions have seen a profit of $845,300, whereas short positions have incurred a loss of $26.0975 million.

Notably, a whale address, 0x0ddf..02, has taken a 3x full-margin short position on BTC at a price of $67,992.10, currently facing an unrealized loss of $10.1479 million.

data.title: Polymarket Traders Profit from Paris Weather Anomalies Amid Tampering Allegations
data.description: Two Polymarket accounts gained approximately $37,000 from Paris weather contracts following unusual temperature increases at Charles de Gaulle Airport Station on April 6 and 15. According to NS3.AI, BFMTV and Bubblemaps reported that the temperature readings temporarily rose above the surrounding conditions. Bubblemaps noted that one trader purchased NO shares on 18°C just before the temperature spike on April 15. Meteo France has reportedly lodged a police complaint concerning alleged tampering with its automated data systems.

data.title: Ethereum Faces Bearish RSI Divergence Amid Whale Accumulation
data.description: Ethereum has experienced a second bearish RSI divergence following an 8.88% correction last week. According to NS3.AI, this market movement coincides with an increase in whale holdings, which rose from 123.75 million ETH on April 19 to 123.91 million ETH by April 22.

data.title: India Refutes Claims of Crypto Payment for Strait of Hormuz Passage
data.description: India has dismissed reports suggesting that the tanker Sanmar Herald made payments in cash or cryptocurrency to Iran for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz. According to NS3.AI, the Indian shipping ministry labeled the allegations as fake news. Sanmar Shipping, the company operating the tanker, also confirmed that no such payment incident took place. MARISKS had previously cautioned about scammers demanding Bitcoin or Tether for fraudulent transit clearances.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Former OpenAI Engineer Launches AI Hardware Company Blackstar Computers
data.description:
Former OpenAI Codex team engineer Daniel Edrisian has announced his departure from the company to establish Blackstar Computers, an AI hardware firm. According to Odaily, Blackstar Computers has successfully completed a $12 million seed funding round led by Abstract, with participation from SV Angel, Naval Ravikant, Chapter One, and Timeless.

Blackstar Computers aims to redefine computing devices by innovating across hardware, software, and interaction layers. Edrisian emphasized that while software development has reached maturity, enhancing human-AI interaction requires a fundamental overhaul at the operating system level. The company currently has a team of approximately eight members located in San Francisco and Shenzhen, and has yet to release any public products.

data.title: Uzbekistan Exempts Mining Income from Tax Until 2035 with New Initiative
data.description: Uzbekistan has introduced the Besqala Mining Valley in Karakalpakstan, offering a tax exemption on mining income until 2035. According to NS3.AI, the initiative allows residents to utilize renewable energy sources for cryptocurrency mining. Mined cryptocurrencies can be sold on both local and international exchanges, with the stipulation that sale proceeds are transferred to banks within Uzbekistan. This regime is exclusively available to legal entities that are registered and operating in Karakalpakstan.

data.title: Philippine Central Bank's Rate Hike Decision Not Unanimously Approved
data.description: The Philippine Central Bank recently announced that its decision to raise interest rates was not unanimously agreed upon by its board members. According to Jin10, the policy move reflects differing opinions among the bank's officials regarding the current economic conditions and the appropriate monetary response. The central bank's decision comes amid ongoing discussions about inflationary pressures and economic growth in the region. The lack of consensus highlights the complexity of balancing economic stability with growth objectives.

data.title: France's April Composite PMI Falls Short of Expectations
data.description: France's composite Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) for April recorded a preliminary value of 47.6, according to Jin10. This figure is below the anticipated 48.6 and the previous month's reading of 48.8. The PMI is a key indicator of economic health, with values below 50 suggesting contraction in the sector.

data.title: Blackstar, founded by a former OpenAI engineer, has raised $12 million in seed funding.
data.description: Former OpenAI Codex engineer Daniel Edrisian has announced his departure to found AI hardware company Blackstar Computers. The company has completed a $12 million seed funding round, led by Abstract, with participation from SV Angel, Naval Ravikant, Chapter One, and Timeless. Blackstar positions itself as a new type of computing device, planning to reshape the computing experience from hardware, software, and interaction perspectives. Edrisian stated that while software development is now mature, further improvements in human-AI interaction require innovation at the operating system level. The company currently has approximately eight employees, located in San Francisco and Shenzhen, and has not yet launched any publicly available products.

data.title: France's Private Sector Activity Contracts at Fastest Pace in 14 Months
data.description: On April 23, a preliminary survey revealed that France's private sector business activity contracted at the fastest pace in 14 months. According to Jin10, this decline was driven by a downturn in the services sector due to the Iran war, while robust growth in manufacturing failed to offset the impact. The services PMI index fell to 46.5 in April from 48.8 in March, marking the lowest level since February 2025. Conversely, the manufacturing PMI index rose to 52.8 in April from 50.0 in March. Factory orders increased for the first time in nearly four years as customers made advance purchases in anticipation of supply shortages and price hikes. Joe Hayes, Chief Economist at S&P Global Market Intelligence, noted, "As expected, manufacturing inflation levels rose further in April due to rising raw material costs, increased transportation fees, and supply bottlenecks driving up prices. Service-oriented businesses are also feeling the pressure from rising transportation costs."

data.title: Blockchain's Appeal: Genuine Interest or Quick Profits?
data.description:
The allure of blockchain technology continues to captivate many, raising questions about whether the interest is driven by genuine enthusiasm or the pursuit of quick profits. According to PANews, the rapid growth of blockchain has attracted a diverse group of individuals and organizations, each with varying motivations. Some are genuinely interested in the technological advancements and potential applications of blockchain, while others are primarily focused on the financial gains it can offer.

Blockchain technology, known for its decentralized nature and ability to facilitate secure transactions, has been embraced by industries ranging from finance to supply chain management. Its potential to revolutionize traditional systems has sparked widespread interest and investment. However, the promise of substantial financial returns has also led to speculative behavior, with some individuals prioritizing short-term gains over long-term development.

The debate over the true motivations behind blockchain adoption is ongoing. While some argue that the technology's transformative potential is the main driver, others believe that the prospect of quick profits is overshadowing genuine interest. This dichotomy is evident in the varying approaches to blockchain projects, with some focusing on innovation and others on maximizing financial returns.

As blockchain continues to evolve, the balance between genuine interest and financial motivation remains a critical factor in its development. Understanding the underlying motivations can help shape the future of blockchain and ensure that its potential is fully realized.

data.title: Telegram Web3 console Conso raises over ten million US dollars, led by IDG Capital.
data.description: Conso App announced the completion of a funding round exceeding ten million US dollars, led by IDG Capital, an early core investor in crypto companies such as Coinbase and Circle. Conso is a Web3 client built on the Telegram API, allowing users to discover high-value content, send and receive community red envelopes, and monitor the movement of "smart money" tokens—all within a single app—all in one place. As a significant milestone in its ecosystem development, Conso became the first third-party client to co-host an ecosystem summit with TON, jointly hosting the "TON AI Builders Day & Telegram AI Ecosystem Co-creators Summit" on April 23. This round of funding will primarily be used to accelerate the "Conso Creator & Builder Program," initially focusing on supporting high-quality Web3 bloggers to achieve cross-platform monetization. Meanwhile, ordinary users can also earn Conso Points through daily interactions.

data.title: Data: The top 3 CEXs by XAU contract trading volume in the past 24 hours are Binance, Bitget, and OKX.
data.description: According to CoinGlass data, XAU is currently trading at $4716.22, down 1.01% in the last 24 hours. Affected by market volatility, the total trading volume of XAU contracts across all exchanges reached $2.064 billion in the last 24 hours, a decrease of 22.29% compared to the previous period. Among centralized exchanges, liquidity is mainly concentrated on Binance, Bitget, and OKX, with their 24-hour trading volumes ranking in the top three at $1.297 billion, $293 million, and $139 million respectively.

data.title: Philippine Central Bank Projects Inflation to Stay at 5% for Most of the Year
data.description: The Philippine Central Bank has projected that inflation will remain at 5% for the majority of this year. According to Jin10, this forecast reflects ongoing economic conditions and monetary policy adjustments aimed at stabilizing prices. The bank's outlook suggests that inflationary pressures are expected to persist, influenced by various domestic and international factors. Policymakers are closely monitoring these developments to ensure economic stability and growth.

data.title: Meme Coin PENGU Experiences Significant Surge in Market Value
data.description: The market value of the Ethereum-based meme coin PENGU has rapidly surpassed $7 million, currently standing at $4.66 million, marking an increase of over 1600 times within a day. According to ChainCatcher, the project claims to be one of the early creators of the Pudgy Penguin NFT series and has launched PenguLegacy on Ethereum, airdropping 50% of the token supply to current Pudgy Penguin holders. ChainCatcher advises users to be cautious, as meme coin prices can be highly volatile.

data.title: France's April Business Confidence Index Falls Short of Expectations
data.description: France's business confidence index for April, as reported by INSEE, registered at 94. According to Jin10, this figure fell short of the anticipated 96 and was a decline from the previous value of 97.

data.title: France's April Manufacturing Confidence Index Matches Expectations
data.description: France's manufacturing confidence index for April, as reported by INSEE, stood at 100, meeting expectations and showing an improvement from the previous value of 99. According to Jin10, this indicates a positive sentiment in the manufacturing sector, reflecting stability and potential growth in the industry.

data.title: Philippine Central Bank: Inflation Outlook Worsens Due to Middle East Conflict
data.description: The Philippine Central Bank has reported a deterioration in the country's inflation outlook, attributing the change to ongoing conflicts in the Middle East. According to Jin10, the geopolitical tensions have led to increased uncertainty in global markets, impacting commodity prices and supply chains. The bank emphasized the need for vigilance in monitoring these developments, as they could have significant implications for the Philippine economy. The central bank is considering potential policy adjustments to mitigate the adverse effects on inflation and ensure economic stability.

data.title: Japan's Central Bank Expected to Raise Interest Rates, Analyst Says
data.description: On April 23, MFS Investment Management analyst Carl Ang stated that the Bank of Japan is expected to increase its policy rate from the current 0.75% to 1% in June. According to Jin10, Ang mentioned that the central bank plans to continue raising rates every six months until reaching a terminal rate of 1.75%. He noted that Japan's new consumer inflation indicator shows underlying price growth slightly below the bank's 2% target, which could justify further rate hikes. Additionally, Ang highlighted that the recent output gap has been revised from negative to positive, indicating that Japan's economic activity level has surpassed its potential.
